brand.name: Chanel
brand.description: French luxury house founded by Gabrielle 'Coco' Chanel. Handbags manufactured primarily in France, with some production in Italy and Spain depending on era and category.

style.name: Classic Flap
style.redesign_notes: 1983: Karl Lagerfeld introduced the interlocking CC turn-lock and the iconic quilted-leather-and-chain combination that defines the modern Classic Flap. The 1955 original (now sold as '2.55') used a rectangular clasp, not the CC turn-lock.
style.description: Chanel's signature quilted flap bag, distinguished from the 2.55 reissue by its CC turn-lock closure (vs. the 2.55's mademoiselle rectangular lock).

variants.authentication_markers: Double flap interior, CC turn-lock engraving crisp and even, chain feels substantial not hollow
variants.notes: Used as the primary worked example (FW 2019). Price-history sources put the Medium Classic Flap US retail at ~$5,800 in 2019 and ~$6,500 in 2020, so the stored $6,500 figure more likely reflects a late-2019/2020 increase than early-2019 retail; treat as approximate (Chanel publishes no official price list). For reference, the Medium's documented US trajectory: ~$4,900 (2016), $5,800 (2019), ~$6,500 (2020), $7,800 (Feb 2021), $8,800 (2022), >$10,000 (2023), ~$10,200 (2024), $11,300 (2025). NOTE: the seed loader does not currently import a price_history table, so these are recorded here as a note rather than as price_history rows.

Size archetype added from research. Dimensions ~23 x 14.5 x 6-7 cm (W x H x D); medium confidence (Xupes, PurseBop, Luxury Evermore). The true 'Small' (23 cm) is distinct from the ~25.5 cm size resellers call Medium (M/L), which is variant 0.
Size archetype added from research. Dimensions ~30 x 19.5 x 10 cm (W x H x D); medium confidence, very consistent across sources (PurseBop, Xupes, Luxury Evermore, Madison Avenue Couture).
Size archetype added from research. Dimensions ~33 x 23 x 10 cm (W x H x D); medium confidence (PurseBop, Xupes, Luxury Evermore).

production_records.dimension_source_note: Multiple reseller listings (Madison Avenue Couture, Luxury Evermore, eBay authenticated listings) converge on approximately 25.5cm W x 14-15.5cm H x 6.5-7cm D for medium caviar GHW. The schema doc's example used H=15.5cm; corroborating sources lean closer to 14cm H. Minor variance across sources is normal for this measurement (where exactly the bag is measured at - widest point vs. base - is not always specified), so both should be treated as approximate, not exact-to-the-millimeter.
production_records.date_code_format: 8-digit hologram sticker code, first two digits = series/year code. Series 25 corresponds to 2018 per cross-referenced sources.
production_records.stamp_placement: Interior leather patch sewn into the bag, typically on the inner wall near the zip pocket
production_records.stamp_font_notes: CHANEL in serif capitals with PARIS and Made in [Country] beneath; even letter spacing on authentic pieces. Could not independently verify font-level micro-details (kerning measurements, exact typeface name) from primary sources - treat stamp_font_notes as a general pattern, not a forensic-grade spec.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Hologram sticker has an X-shaped cut to prevent removal/reapplication without visible damage; covered by clear tape printed vertically with 'CHANEL' and a dark line down one side, with iridescent flecks. Numeral '0' in the serial has a strikethrough; numeral '1' has small serifs/feet. Rainbow shimmer visible when tilted under light.
production_records.sources: Xupes Chanel Date Codes Guide; FASHIONPHILE Chanel Serial Codes Decoded; Madison Avenue Couture, Luxury Evermore listings
production_records.correction_note: The database-schema.md example doc states 'Series 25 hologram introduced 2018' and attributes year_range 2018-2021 to it. Independent sources (Xupes) corroborate Series 25 = 2018 specifically, but indicate the 8-digit-code system as a whole (not just Series 25) ran through 2021/2022, with Series 26-31 covering 2019-2021. The original schema doc's year_range for the SPECIFIC Series-25 code should be narrowed to approximately 2018 only, not stretched to 2018-2021 - that broader range describes the sticker-system era, not the Series 25 code itself. Flagging this as a correction to the existing schema doc's worked example.

production_records.dimension_source_note: Small (23 cm) dimensions ~23 W x 14.5 H x 6-7 D cm; depth 6 vs 7 varies by source. Snippet-level (Xupes, PurseBop, Luxury Evermore).
production_records.date_code_format: Same Chanel serial/microchip system as the Medium record; not size-specific.
production_records.stamp_placement: Interior leather patch near the zip pocket.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Dimensions ~23 x 14.5 x 6.5 cm (W x H x D).
production_records.sources: Xupes Chanel size guide; PurseBop; Luxury Evermore

production_records.dimension_source_note: Jumbo dimensions ~30 W x 19.5 H x 10 D cm; consistent across sources. Snippet-level.
production_records.date_code_format: Same Chanel serial/microchip system as the Medium record.
production_records.stamp_placement: Interior leather patch near the zip pocket.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Dimensions ~30 x 19.5 x 10 cm (W x H x D).
production_records.sources: PurseBop; Xupes; Luxury Evermore; Madison Avenue Couture

production_records.dimension_source_note: Maxi dimensions ~33 W x 23 H x 10 D cm; consistent across sources. Snippet-level.
production_records.date_code_format: Same Chanel serial/microchip system as the Medium record.
production_records.stamp_placement: Interior leather patch near the zip pocket.
production_records.known_authentication_markers: Dimensions ~33 x 23 x 10 cm (W x H x D).

known_color_combinations.year_range: long-running, seen across multiple years including 2019
known_color_combinations.authentication_notes: Burgundy interior with black caviar exterior is one of the most common, well-documented combinations. Burgundy should read as deep wine/oxblood, not bright red or purple-toned. This combination appears repeatedly across reseller listings and collector forum threads, giving it higher confidence than most combination claims.

Could not find a single verified authentic example of black caviar exterior paired with a bright/fire-engine red interior lining in any reseller catalog, collector forum, or authentication guide reviewed. This is consistent with the original schema doc's claim, but I was not able to find a source that explicitly states 'this combination was never produced' as a documented fact (as opposed to absence of evidence). Downgrading confidence from the schema doc's implicit 'high' to medium - absence of a sighting is suggestive but not the same as a brand-confirmed negative.

Black caviar with silver hardware is a documented standard pairing (alongside the gold-hardware version). Interior color is era-dependent (left null). Source: Madison Avenue Couture, 1stdibs (snippet-level).

Black LAMBSKIN (vs caviar) Classic Flap, offered with gold or silver hardware. Lambskin flaps are commonly documented with a burgundy interior, though this varies by era (not a hard rule). Source: PurseForum threads, Madison Avenue Couture (snippet-level).
Ruthenium hardware appears on metallic/goatskin and seasonal Classic Flap pieces. Source: mygemma.com hardware metal-types guide, Madison Avenue Couture (snippet-level).

carry_methods.notes: Chain strap can be doubled for a shorter handheld/shoulder carry or worn long; medium flap chain strap drop commonly cited in the 23-43cm adjustable-by-doubling range depending on listing source.
Chain is not length-adjustable via buckle; true crossbody fit depends heavily on wearer torso height. Commonly reported as difficult for wearers above approx. 5'6".
Medium Classic Flap has no top handle; only the chain strap is provided.

fits.notes: Reported by multiple owner/collector sources as fitting in the open slip pockets more easily than the zip pocket. Not independently lab-verified by this research, based on aggregated anecdotal owner reports rather than a single authoritative source.

Medium flap's structured, narrow opening and curved silhouette are commonly reported as too small/narrow for an iPad Mini to lie flat. Based on general consensus across handbag-sizing discussions rather than a single verified measurement test.
Smaller than an iPad Mini and more plausible, but not independently verified by direct measurement against this exact variant's opening dimensions.

interior_storage.authentication_notes: Commonly repeated claim across authentication guides is that modern (post-2008-ish) medium Classic Flaps have a single zip pocket on the back wall, with two zip pockets associated with vintage/pre-2008 configurations or signaling a fake on a modern bag. I could not find a single primary source that pins this transition to an exact year with full confidence - multiple guides reference hardware/hallmark changes around Cruise 2008 but pocket-count specifics were not consistently corroborated across sources in this research pass. Treat the 'pre/post-2008' framing as a widely-repeated claim, not independently verified to my satisfaction. Confidence downgraded from the schema doc's implicit certainty.
Standard configuration reported across multiple reseller listings for medium caviar flaps of this era.

serial_tags.format: 8-digit number; first two digits = series code
serial_tags.placement: Interior leather patch sewn into the bag, near the zip pocket
serial_tags.material: sticker, covered with clear security tape
serial_tags.how_to_read: First two digits indicate a series/year code. CAVEAT: the precise series-to-year mapping (24=2017, 25=2018, 26-27=2019, 28=2019-2020, 29=2020, 30=2020-2021, 31=2021) is RESELLER-RECONSTRUCTED and approximate -- it is widely repeated but not Chanel-published, and exact boundaries vary by source, so treat it as a guide, not an authoritative table. The HIGH-confidence, cross-corroborated system-level facts are: serial stickers introduced 1984 (earliest 6-digit, 1XXXXX); transitioned to 7-digit in 1986; 8-digit format used ~2005-2021 (10XXXXXX up to 31XXXXXX); 31XXXXXX is the last serial code used on handbags; SLG stickers continued to 32XXXXXX in 2021-2022.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: Sticker has an X-shaped cut into it so it tears if peeled. Clear tape over the sticker is printed vertically with 'CHANEL' and has a dark line down one side plus faint iridescent flecks visible when tilted. Numeral '0' has a strikethrough; numeral '1' has small serifs/feet on authentic stickers. A flat, non-iridescent sticker or blurry/smudged printing is a red flag. Do NOT rely on the exact series-year mapping above to date a bag to a single year -- the digit-count era (6/7/8-digit) and the 1984/1986 milestones are the reliable, citable anchors.
serial_tags.sources: Xupes Chanel Date Codes Guide; FASHIONPHILE Chanel Serial Codes Decoded; italystation.com hologram guide; luxbags.fr serial-sticker era guide; romestation.ca; coutureusa.com

serial_tags.format: 8-character alphanumeric code on a small metal plate with laser-engraved CC logo, color-matched to hardware; codes are non-sequential and do not encode production year
serial_tags.placement: Stitched into lining, flap, or inside a pocket depending on style
serial_tags.material: metal plate with embedded NFC microchip
serial_tags.year_range: Introduced April 2021 onward, replacing the sticker system
serial_tags.how_to_read: Code is not human-decodable for year/factory information; chip is read only via Chanel's proprietary in-store NFC equipment, not by the public or resellers.
serial_tags.authentication_notes: Does not apply to the FW2019 worked example, included for completeness since this directly affects post-2021 variants and the 'no physical card' provenance claim.
serial_tags.sources: Madison Avenue Couture: 'Chanel Bags Switch from Serial Stickers to Microchips in 2021'; cocoapproved.com; FASHIONPHILE; Lux Second Chance; Bag Religion microchip guide

locks_and_keys.authentication_notes: The Classic Flap closes via the CC turn-lock only; it has no separate padlock, no clochette, and no keys. This is consistent across all sources reviewed and is a well-established, uncontroversial fact in the Chanel collector community.

provenance_packaging.description: Black flannel dust bag with white CHANEL serif logo and white drawstring cord
provenance_packaging.format_by_year: Dust bag style/font has changed across eras; specific micro-variations by year not independently verified in this pass.
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: Dust bag alone is not authentication-grade evidence; fakes commonly include passable dust bags.
provenance_packaging.value_impact_if_missing: Minor resale value impact; full box-and-bag sets typically command modestly higher resale prices but this is not authentication-relevant.

provenance_packaging.description: For bags sold before the 2021 microchip transition: a white card bearing a serial number matching the bag's hologram sticker.
provenance_packaging.format_by_year: Discontinued April 2021 onward; replaced by embedded NFC microchip plates that cannot be read by the public. Bags sold new from April 2021 forward do not include a physical authenticity card.
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: Authenticity card serial must match the bag's own hologram exactly for cards from the pre-2021 era; mismatched serials are a clear red flag. Post-2021, absence of a card is normal and expected, not suspicious.
provenance_packaging.value_impact_if_missing: For pre-2021 bags, a missing card has some negative resale impact but the bag can still be authenticated via the hologram/serial alone.
provenance_packaging.sources: Madison Avenue Couture; Lux Second Chance; Bag Religion

provenance_packaging.description: Black rigid box with white ribbon and white CHANEL wordmark on the lid
provenance_packaging.authentication_notes: Box presence/condition is not itself authentication-grade evidence.
provenance_packaging.value_impact_if_missing: Minimal impact on authentication; modest resale impact.

price_history.notes: Medium Classic Flap US retail. Snippet-sourced price trajectory (Luxury Evermore, Fashionista, Baghunter); not a resale observation.
Medium US retail, 2020 (matches the stored variant retail_price_original).
Medium US retail crossed $10,000 in 2023 (figure is approximate, '>$10,000').

sources: /Users/ariellecoambes/Documents/luxury-catalog/docs/database-schema.md (existing vetted schema example, used as starting template, corrections noted inline)

research_notes.key_correction_to_existing_schema_doc: The schema doc's worked example states Series 25 hologram covers 2018-2021. Cross-referencing Xupes and FASHIONPHILE: Series 25 = 2018 specifically; series 26-31 cover 2019-2021. The 2018-2021 range in the original doc appears to conflate 'the sticker era broadly' with 'Series 25 specifically' - these should be treated as two different claims and the production_records entry above narrows this.
low_confidence_areas_flagged: Exact retail price for FW2019 medium caviar GHW ($6,500 figure not pinned to a verifiable invoice; multiple Chanel price increases occurred in 2019)
Pre/post-2008 one-zip-pocket vs two-zip-pocket claim - widely repeated in secondary sources but I could not independently verify with a primary/authoritative source in this research pass
Fits data (iPhone/iPad Mini/Kindle) - based on aggregated anecdotal owner reports, not direct measurement verification
Exact dimensions vary slightly by source (14-15.5cm height) - likely due to inconsistent measurement methodology across resellers, not a true production variance
'Never produced' color combination claims rely on absence of evidence rather than a brand-confirmed negative statement
